confirmed.summary: The FR-006/SC-003 token-free smoke test runs in ZERO CI shards, yet the acceptance matrix records it as CI-verified
confirmed.evidence: tests/ci/test_sonarcloud_branch_review.py carries no pytest marker (grep: only test_sonar_project_version.py and test_sonar_projectversion_wired.py have `pytestmark = pytest.mark.fast`). The only CI job that collects tests/ci is fast-tests-core-misc's core-misc shard (.github/workflows/ci-quality.yml:1428-1453 empty positional + ignore list that does NOT include tests/ci), which filters `-m "fast and not windows_ci"` (:1471). Empirically confirmed: `pytest tests/ci -m "fast and not windows_ci" --collect-only` collects ONLY the 2 version files (14 tests); the smoke test's 20 tests are deselected. The integration-core-misc shards carry the right marker family (:1667 `git_repo or integration or architectural`) but use explicit positional path lists (:1622-1647 `misc` shard, etc.) that exclude tests/ci, so `pytest tests/ci -m "...(git_repo or integration or architectural)" --collect-only` collects nothing. Every other workflow pytest run requires a marker the smoke test lacks or an explicit non-tests/ci positional (verified across ci-quality.yml, ci-windows.yml, drift-detector.yml, release.yml). Yet acceptance-matrix.json FR-006 asserts `"The tool has a token-free... smoke test that passes in CI"` pass, verified_by reviewer-renata, and spec.md SC-003 claims `its smoke test passes in CI`. The smoke test passes ONLY when run locally (20/20 green here). Its load-bearing read-only-invariant guards (test_every_runtime_request_is_a_read_only_get, test_script_source_is_statically_read_only) are therefore never enforced in CI.
confirmed.recommendation: Mark test_sonarcloud_branch_review.py with `integration` (it is ~42s, subprocess+bash — legitimately NOT `fast`), AND add `tests/ci` to the integration-core-misc `misc` shard's positional paths (ci-quality.yml:1622-1647) so the `(integration...)` selector collects it. Then correct/re-verify acceptance-matrix FR-006. Add an architectural guard asserting no test file under tests/ci is orphaned from every CI shard's (selector × positional) matrix.

verdict.reasoning:
Verified against the live repo and empirically reproduced. Every load-bearing claim holds:

MARKER: tests/ci/test_sonarcloud_branch_review.py carries NO pytest marker (grep + read confirm); only the two version files have `pytestmark = pytest.mark.fast`. No conftest or pytest.ini/addopts auto-applies fast/integration to tests/ci (tests/conftest.py:199 adds only windows-skip/quarantine-skip/arch_shard; per-dir marker conftests are scoped to glossary/charter/status).

EMPIRICAL (ran in project venv): `pytest tests/ci -m "fast and not windows_ci" --collect-only` -> 14 collected / 20 deselected (only the 2 version files); `pytest tests/ci -m "git_repo or integration or architectural" --collect-only` -> 0 collected / 34 deselected; the smoke file has exactly 20 tests. So the smoke test's 20 tests are collected by NO CI selector.

WORKFLOW TOPOLOGY: ci-quality.yml:1428-1453 core-misc shard is empty-positional with an ignore list that does NOT include tests/ci but filters `-m "fast and not windows_ci"` (:1471) -> scans tests/ci but deselects the marker-less smoke test. Integration shards (:1622-1647 misc + siblings) use explicit positional path lists excluding tests/ci and require (git_repo or integration or architectural) (:1667), which the smoke test lacks. I exhaustively enumerated every pytest invocation across all five pytest-bearing workflows: every pure-negative-marker run (597, 1006, 1510, 2621) uses an explicit non-tests/ci positional; the only tests/ci-inclusive scopes (core-misc empty positional; :2417 `pytest tests/`) require a positive marker (fast, or unit/contract) the smoke test does not carry. ci-windows/drift-detector/release all use explicit non-tests/ci positionals or windows_ci. No run collects the smoke test.

ACCEPTANCE: acceptance-matrix.json FR-006 = pass, verified_by reviewer-renata, description asserts "smoke test that passes in CI", but its evidence is a LOCAL direct-path run (`env -u SONAR_TOKEN pytest .../test_sonarcloud_branch_review.py -> 20 passed`). spec.md SC-003 (line 70), line 26, FR-006 (line 44) all claim "passes/runs in CI". The most recent commit only re-touched NFR-001; the FR-006 CI-collection gap is uncorrected. Both load-bearing read-only guards (test_script_source_is_statically_read_only:255, test_every_runtime_request_is_a_read_only_get:265) exist and are therefore never enforced in CI.

This is a genuine witnessed-vs-static dormant mask: the test was witnessed passing locally by direct path and recorded as CI-verified, while the static CI topology never collects it. Adversarial counter-arguments (marker auto-applied elsewhere; FR-006 actually referring to the version files; already-handled by last commit) were each checked and refuted. The finding's only imprecision ("only core-misc collects tests/ci") understates reality and does not weaken the conclusion.

confirmed.summary: Neither scripts/ci/** nor tests/ci/** is registered in any CI path-filter group, so edits to the new tool or its tests trigger no test job
confirmed.evidence: The dorny/paths-filter groups in .github/workflows/ci-quality.yml:142-346 map many src/ and tests/ roots but list neither `scripts/ci/**` nor `tests/ci/**` (the `release` group includes `scripts/release/**` and `.github/workflows/scripts/**` but not `scripts/ci/**`). The fail-closed `unmatched` catch-all only fires for `any_src` = `src/**` (:346), and scripts/ci is not under src/. Consequence: a future PR that edits ONLY scripts/ci/sonarcloud_branch_review.sh (e.g. introduces a mutating call or a SONAR_TOKEN dependency) or ONLY scripts/ci/sonar_project_version.py (e.g. breaks version extraction) triggers no test job at all — DIR-030's producer-conformance gate does not cover the very surfaces this mission adds.
confirmed.recommendation: Add `scripts/ci/**` and `tests/ci/**` to a path-filter group (e.g. core_misc) whose triggered shard actually runs these tests, so a change to the tool or the extractor re-runs their guards. Pair with the marker/positional fix in finding 1 so the routed job collects them.

verdict.reasoning: Finding withstands adversarial refutation against the live tree. Verified in .github/workflows/ci-quality.yml: (1) the dorny/paths-filter block (lines 142-346) registers neither scripts/ci/** nor tests/ci/**; the release group lists scripts/release/** and .github/workflows/scripts/** (170-171) but not scripts/ci/**; (2) the fail-closed unmatched catch-all computes only over any_src=src/** (line 346, src-only union 363-368), so a tests/ci-only change yields unmatched=false and every per-module test job skips; (3) scripts/ci/** is also absent from the workflow-level on.pull_request.paths / on.push.paths (14-64) — so a scripts/ci-only PR does not even trigger the workflow (stronger than the finding claims). Refutations failed: the sole always-on broad job unit-contract-residual (2407-2421) selects `(unit or contract) and not fast…`, which excludes the two fast-marked tests/ci files and the unmarked smoke test alike; arch-adversarial collects tests/adversarial|architectural|architecture|lint, not tests/ci; fast-tests-core-misc's core-misc shard would collect tests/ci (its ignore list omits tests/ci) but only fires when core_misc/composite groups are true (a src change), never for an isolated scripts/ci or tests/ci edit; and the nightly sonar step hard-errors only on EMPTY extractor output (3116), missing wrong-but-nonempty versions and providing no guard for the shell tool's read-only invariant, and it runs nightly not on the PR. The mission adds exactly these surfaces (scripts/ci/sonar_project_version.py, scripts/ci/sonarcloud_branch_review.sh, tests/ci/*) and FR-006/SC-003 require the smoke test to run in CI; the analysis-report (findings: []) never flags this registration gap, so it is not already-handled. Evidence line numbers are accurate; not stale, not misread. Medium is appropriate: a genuine DIR-030 producer-conformance topology hole that lets future isolated edits to these surfaces ship without their guards (and, for scripts/ci, without the workflow running at all), yet not a current functional break (src-touching PRs over-cover the fast tests) — so not high/critical, and not clearly low given the unguarded read-only invariant plus the mission's explicit runs-in-CI requirement. Only nit: 'triggers no test job at all' is slightly loose for the tests/ci case (unit-contract-residual runs but collects nothing relevant); exactly true for the scripts/ci case; substance stands.

confirmed.summary: Gate-4 issue-matrix still certifies #2422 as non-terminal 'in-mission' while the mission is merged and the acceptance-matrix certifies the same work as pass
confirmed.evidence: issue-matrix.md:8 `| #2422 | ... | in-mission | WP03 ... must reach `fixed` before mission `done` |`; its own legend at line 13 defines in-mission as non-terminal. Contradicted by status.json (WP03 lane="done", summary.done=3) and acceptance-matrix.json:28-45 (FR-003 + FR-004 pass_fail="pass", verified_by reviewer-renata). All committed on HEAD (git status clean).
confirmed.recommendation: Update the #2422 row verdict to `fixed` with an evidence ref to WP03 / docs/guides/coverage-signals.md, noting C-002 was discharged by documentation (file-set + philosophy differ, no sources/exclusions misconfig). Leaving it 'in-mission' makes the mission's own per-issue certification assert the coverage concern is unresolved at merge time.

verdict.reasoning:
The finding withstands adversarial scrutiny against live HEAD (working tree clean). Every cited line verifies exactly: issue-matrix.md:8 marks #2422 as `in-mission` with "must reach fixed before mission done"; its legend at :13 defines `in-mission` as non-terminal ("being fixed by a LATER WP ... must reach a terminal verdict before mission done"). This is contradicted by status.json (WP03 lane=done, actor=merge, summary.done=3 — mission squash-merged at commit 5f3e3e1ab) and acceptance-matrix.json:28-45 (FR-003, the exact #2422 coverage-scope/WP03/coverage-signals.md work, and FR-004 both pass_fail=pass, verified_by reviewer-renata). WP/issue/deliverable mapping is identical across both artifacts.

Commit archaeology confirms staleness, not misreading: issue-matrix was committed at 16:58 (02aa6e8), a git ancestor of the 17:24 merge, and never updated afterward; no later commit touches the #2422 verdict, and retrospective.yaml does not acknowledge or discharge it (so not already-handled).

Adversarial counters rejected: (1) "acceptance overall_verdict=pending means mission isn't done, so in-mission is fine" — rejected because the code is merged, all WP lanes are done via actor=merge, retrospective is captured, and `in-mission` means "being fixed by a LATER WP" while WP03 was the final WP and is done+reviewed-pass; there is no later WP, so the row is factually false regardless. (2) "ideal terminal verdict may not be exactly `fixed`" — even if the resolution is by-documentation/by-design (C-002 found no misconfig), `in-mission` is the one value reserved for in-progress work, so the drift holds independent of the replacement token.

This is a genuine cross-artifact conceptual-alignment drift (DIR-032): the Gate-4 per-issue certification asserts a scoped issue is unresolved/non-terminal while the mission merges and the acceptance-matrix certifies the same work as pass. Severity kept at medium — it is an audit/governance-integrity contradiction in the very artifact meant to settle per-issue disposition at merge, but has no functional/user/code impact and is a one-line fix, placing it at the low/medium boundary rather than high.

confirmed.summary: FR-003 coverage doc claims 'reproduce every figure' but the mission's own tool cannot emit the load-bearing absolute denominators it cites
confirmed.evidence: docs/guides/coverage-signals.md:69 states 'You can reproduce every figure yourself:' then lists only `... coverage`, `... quality-gate`, `... version`. But `cmd_coverage` (scripts/ci/sonarcloud_branch_review.sh:112-119) queries `metricKeys=coverage,new_coverage` and prints only those two percentages. The doc's core quantitative evidence — `lines_to_cover = 95,111`, `uncovered_lines = 50,090`, `new_lines_to_cover = 80,038` (coverage-signals.md:90-99) and `~247` / `~969` / `1305` file counts (coverage-signals.md:59-63) — is not produced by any subcommand (`uncovered` requires a per-file arg; there is no component-tree/file-count query). FR-005 explicitly authored the tool so this investigation would route through 'one canonical, testable tool instead of ad-hoc API calls', yet the denominators driving the argument came from ad-hoc calls the tool doesn't expose and a reader cannot re-derive them.
confirmed.recommendation: Either extend the `coverage` subcommand's metricKeys to include `lines_to_cover,uncovered_lines,new_lines_to_cover` and add a component-tree/file-count query so the tool actually reproduces the cited figures, or amend the doc: soften 'every figure' to the percentages/version/gate the tool does reproduce, and record the exact `/api/measures/component` + `/api/components/tree` queries used to obtain the absolute line/file counts.

verdict.reasoning:
Every factual claim in the finding is verified against the live, committed artifacts.

CONFIRMED FACTS:
1. docs/guides/coverage-signals.md:69 states "You can reproduce every figure yourself:" followed by exactly three subcommands (coverage, quality-gate, version). Line 180 doubles the claim: the tool "reproduces every number above." Both attribute the whole reconciliation to the tool.
2. scripts/ci/sonarcloud_branch_review.sh cmd_coverage (112-119) queries only metricKeys=coverage,new_coverage — no lines_to_cover/uncovered_lines/new_lines_to_cover at project level. cmd_uncovered (121-128) hardcodes component=${PROJECT_KEY}:${file}, requires a file arg, cannot query the project aggregate, and never queries new_lines_to_cover. No files/ncloc/component_tree query exists anywhere in the tool.
3. new_lines_to_cover (doc:99, "80,038") appears nowhere in scripts/ or tests/ — the tool literally cannot emit it. The 1305 Sonar-indexed-file count (and derived ~969/~247) is a SonarCloud figure with no producing subcommand. The doc records no raw API queries as a fallback.
4. The doc is the final committed WP03 deliverable (squash-merged, clean tree). Not stale, not a pending companion change. The Known-caveats section does not address this.

MISSION-CONTEXT AMPLIFIER: US3 (spec:25) built the tool specifically so investigations route through "one canonical, testable tool instead of ad-hoc API calls," and the spec Edge Case (spec:29) prescribes file-set determination via GET /api/measures/component_tree — a query the shipped tool never exposes. So the load-bearing denominators came from exactly the ad-hoc calls the mission meant to eliminate, and the doc conceals that. This is a direct tension with the mission's own stated rationale.

ADVERSARIAL STEELMAN REJECTED: "Every figure = only the percentages" fails because the section is titled "Why the numbers differ (the evidence)" and the central argument explicitly rests on the absolute counts (~95k lines vs your diff). The doc's purpose (FR-003/SC-002) is a verifiable reconciliation a reviewer can trust without re-litigating; the numbers change every nightly, so a future reader genuinely needs to re-derive and cannot via the documented path. This is not an FR-005 tool-defect (the tool meets its coverage-metrics + per-file-uncovered contract); it is correctly categorized as an FR-003 documentation-fidelity gap.

SEVERITY: medium retained. It is a docs fidelity/evidence-integrity defect (no code/gate break, so not higher), but the doubled overclaim plus the contradiction with the "canonical tool not ad-hoc calls" rationale lifts it above a cosmetic low nit. The recommendation (extend coverage metricKeys + add a component-tree/file-count query, or soften "every figure" and record the exact /api/measures/component + /api/components/tree queries) is sound and actionable.

The finding withstands scrutiny on every checkable claim.

confirmed.summary: issue-matrix still marks #2422 'in-mission' and tasks.md WP03 checkboxes are unticked, contradicting WP03=done and passing acceptance criteria at a pre-merge gate
confirmed.evidence: kitty-specs/sonar-qa-config-remediation-01KWYCX7/issue-matrix.md:8 lists #2422 verdict `in-mission` with the rule `must reach fixed before mission done`, but status.json shows WP03 lane `done` (all 3 WPs done, actor `merge`) and acceptance-matrix.json FR-003/FR-004 are `pass`. tasks.md:31-32 still shows `- [ ] T005` / `- [ ] T006` unchecked despite the WP03 deliverable (docs/guides/coverage-signals.md) having landed and its activity log (tasks/WP03-coverage-scope-doc.md:56) recording completion. A reviewer keying off issue-matrix.md would wrongly conclude the mission is incomplete.
confirmed.recommendation: Update issue-matrix.md #2422 verdict to `fixed` (evidence: WP03 coverage-signals.md, C-002 discharge) and tick tasks.md T005/T006 before merge so the mission's own trace agrees with status.json.

verdict.reasoning:
The finding withstands adversarial scrutiny against the live committed HEAD of the pr_bound branch feat/sonar-qa-config-remediation. Every claim verified:

1. issue-matrix.md:8 still marks #2422 verdict `in-mission` with its own rule "must reach `fixed` before mission `done`". git log shows it was written once (02aa6e8ef) and never updated to a terminal verdict (02aa6e8ef..HEAD empty for that file). Not stale, not already-handled.
2. status.json shows all 3 WPs `done`, actor `merge` — so the mission is in the `done` state, which trips the issue-matrix's own "before mission done" precondition. A genuine self-contradiction in the FR-037 Gate-4 review artifact.
3. acceptance-matrix.json FR-003/FR-004 both `pass`, explicitly tied to WP03/coverage-signals.md/C-002 discharge of #2422.
4. tasks.md:31-32 T005/T006 are `- [ ]` unticked — and this is stronger than the finding stated: commit 4fd6c12f7 ("mark WP subtasks done", 17:21) ticked both to [x], then the squash merge 5f3e3e1ab reverted them to [ ]. The drift is a regression re-introduced by the coordination squash-merge, never re-applied (working tree clean).
5. docs/guides/coverage-signals.md (11060 bytes) exists in both main and coord worktree; tasks/WP03-coverage-scope-doc.md:56 records completion.

Refutation attempts fail: not stale (current HEAD), not misread (exact line refs), not already-handled (never reconciled; ticks actively reverted). The "pre-merge" framing is valid — the mission squash-merged into the local feat branch which is pr_bound and awaits its PR to main, so this is a legitimate pre-PR trace-consistency catch.

Severity low is correct: the machine-authoritative sources (status.json for lane state, acceptance-matrix.json for acceptance) are both correct and the deliverable shipped and is linked, so the only impact is a human reviewer keying off the stale trace artifacts (issue-matrix.md/tasks.md) forming a wrong "incomplete" conclusion. No functional/code impact. Medium is defensible because issue-matrix.md is a Gate-4 artifact whose own contract is violated, but low is the honest call given correct authoritative state. The recommendation is sound (fixed is the most defensible available terminal verdict; re-tick the checkboxes the squash reverted).

confirmed.summary: tasks.md marks T005/T006 (WP03) unchecked despite WP03 being approved, done, and merged
confirmed.evidence: tasks.md:31-32 `- [ ] T005 ...` / `- [ ] T006 ...` (and WP03 section still described as pending) vs status.events.jsonl (WP03 approved 15:16:23, done/merged 15:24:24) and acceptance-matrix.json FR-003/FR-004 pass. status.events is the authority per the status model, so this is non-blocking, but the committed human-readable task list misrepresents completion.
confirmed.recommendation: Check T005/T006 in tasks.md so the committed dossier is coherent with the authoritative done state.

verdict.reasoning: All load-bearing claims verified against live artifacts. tasks.md:31-32 show `- [ ] T005` / `- [ ] T006` exactly as quoted (working tree, HEAD 664150f31, and coord worktree copy), while WP01/WP02 boxes T001-T004 are all [x] — a genuine asymmetry, not a whole-file convention. status.events.jsonl confirms WP03 in_review→approved at 2026-07-07T15:16:23.703005 (actor user) and approved→done at 15:24:24.068333 (actor merge), matching the finding's timestamps. status.json shows WP03 lane=done (summary done:3), and acceptance-matrix.json shows FR-003 pass and FR-004 pass. So the committed human-readable task list contradicts the authoritative done/merged state. Not misread (line numbers/content exact), not stale (present at HEAD), not already-handled (git status clean, no pending fix). Adversarial git archaeology strengthens rather than refutes it: commit 4fd6c12f7 DID tick T005/T006 to [x], but the squash-merge commit 5f3e3e1ab reverted them to [ ] by folding an older lane tasks.md, and HEAD retains the unchecked state — a persistent, currently-committed drift (lane-squash clobbering coordination-branch dossier edits). Severity correctly stays low: per the status model, status.events.jsonl is the sole authority and tasks.md checkboxes are human-readable cosmetics, so the authoritative state is coherent and this is non-blocking. One minor overstatement in the finding — the parenthetical 'WP03 section still described as pending' has no literal 'pending' prose; the impression comes only from the unchecked boxes — but this does not undermine the core, verified claim. Recommendation to tick T005/T006 is the correct minimal fix.

confirmed.summary: WP03 edited docs/guides/index.md, a surface not declared in its owned_files / create_intent
confirmed.evidence: tasks/WP03-coverage-scope-doc.md frontmatter declares create_intent=[docs/guides/coverage-signals.md] and owned_files=[docs/guides/coverage-signals.md] only; the diff also modifies docs/guides/index.md (FR-004 linkage). The edit is acknowledged post-hoc in acceptance-matrix.json:41 ('single minimal within-docs/guides/ index edit') but never declared in the WP's ownership set.
confirmed.recommendation: Add docs/guides/index.md to WP03's create_intent/owned_files. Benign here (no parallel docs lane), but an undeclared owned surface can cause undetected cross-lane collisions when doc-touching WPs run in parallel.

verdict.reasoning:
Every verifiable claim in the finding holds against the live repo. WP03's commit c1137e5 modifies docs/guides/index.md (2 insertions: a related: frontmatter entry + a guides-list link — exact diff confirmed, and present in the current file at lines 9/30). WP03's frontmatter (tasks/WP03-coverage-scope-doc.md lines 22-26) declares create_intent and owned_files as [docs/guides/coverage-signals.md] only; index.md is in neither, and is also absent from lanes.json lane-c write_scope (the machine-derived collision surface). acceptance-matrix.json:45 acknowledges the edit ('single minimal within-docs/guides/ index edit') but the owned_files set was never updated — so it is acknowledged-but-not-remediated, not stale/handled.

I ran the strongest refutations and they fail. (1) 'authoritative_surface: docs/guides/ already declares it': refuted by the mission's OWN convention — WP01 edits pre-existing .github/workflows/ci-quality.yml (112 prior commits; genuinely pre-existing) and lists it in owned_files but NOT create_intent, DESPITE having authoritative_surface: .github/workflows/. So the directory-level authoritative_surface does not substitute for enumerating edited files in owned_files; the file-level write-set is the collision surface. WP03 is directly inconsistent with WP01: it omitted its edited pre-existing file (index.md) from owned_files. (2) 'The edit was mandated by T006/FR-004': that reduces blame but is exactly why it should have been declared — it does not close the gap. (3) The recommendation is half-imprecise: index.md is an edit, so it belongs in owned_files, not create_intent; but the finding writes 'create_intent/owned_files' and the owned_files half is correct, so this is a wording imperfection, not a refutation.

Severity low is correct and I keep it: benign in this mission (lane-c is solo in parallel_group 1; WP01/WP02 touch .github/scripts/tests, not docs → zero parallel-docs collision here). The stated collision risk is a valid general-case concern that does not materialize in this mission. Net: a real, low-severity boundary/declaration-hygiene finding that withstands scrutiny; only its create_intent wording is slightly imprecise.

confirmed.summary: Tasks ledger and acceptance verdict lag behind the done/approved WP03 state
confirmed.evidence: tasks.md:31-32 lists `- [ ] T005` and `- [ ] T006` as unchecked, while status.json:34-37 records WP03 `lane: done` and acceptance-matrix.json marks FR-003/FR-004 `pass` with the deliverables (docs/guides/coverage-signals.md + the index link) committed. acceptance-matrix.json:5 also still reads `"overall_verdict": "pending"` though every criterion is `pass` and all three WPs are done.
confirmed.recommendation: Tick T005/T006 in tasks.md (or note why they remain open) so the subtask ledger matches the done WP, and reconcile acceptance-matrix `overall_verdict` once this pre-merge gate passes — otherwise a completion audit sees a done mission with open subtasks and a pending verdict.

verdict.reasoning:
Every evidentiary claim verifies exactly against live HEAD (branch feat/sonar-qa-config-remediation, tip 664150f31): tasks.md:31-32 shows T005/T006 as `- [ ]` unchecked; status.json:34-37 shows WP03 lane:done (summary done:3); acceptance-matrix.json has all 6 criteria pass_fail=pass including FR-003/FR-004; acceptance-matrix.json:5 reads overall_verdict:pending; and the WP03 deliverables are committed (docs/guides/coverage-signals.md, 11KB, tracked in HEAD; linked from docs/guides/index.md:30). The evidence is neither stale nor misread.

The inconsistency is genuine, not a workflow no-op: WP01's T001/T002 and WP02's T003/T004 (both done WPs) ARE ticked [x], so WP03 is a real outlier. Git history confirms the mechanism — commit 4fd6c12f7 ('mark WP subtasks done') explicitly ticked T005/T006 to [x], then the squash-merge commit 5f3e3e1ab re-materialized tasks.md from a stale pre-tick state, reverting both to [ ], and no later commit re-ticked them. Mission-dir git status is clean, so it is unremediated at HEAD — not already-handled.

Severity low is appropriate. This is a ledger/audit-consistency drift with no functional or deliverable impact: the authoritative event-sourced status.json correctly records WP03 done and all deliverables exist. The overall_verdict=pending sub-claim is the softest element (a pending verdict is the expected state while this very pre-merge acceptance gate is in flight), but the finding itself frames flipping it as a post-gate to-do rather than overclaiming, and the primary claim (subtask ledger lags the done WP) withstands adversarial scrutiny cleanly.

dropped.summary: Acceptance matrix asserts the shell tool is 'shellcheck clean' but no CI job runs shellcheck — the claim is manual-only and unenforced
dropped.why_refuted:
Every atomic fact in the finding verifies (acceptance-matrix FR-005 says "shellcheck clean"; spec.md:57 C-003 requires shellcheck "if the repo runs it"; no shellcheck runs anywhere in .github/ per exhaustive grep incl. action variants; shellcheck not installed here). But the finding's thesis — a real security/gate-enforcement defect / misleading CI-verified record — does not withstand adversarial scrutiny.

(1) No false representation exists. C-003 is explicitly conditional ("passes shellcheck if the repo runs it") — the spec author pre-disclosed that the repo may not run it. "shellcheck clean" is a true PROPERTY claim about the artifact (run manually/locally, passed), not a claim of CI enforcement; the matrix never says CI or "gate-enforced." The finding reads an enforcement claim into a statement that makes none.

(2) The constraint is satisfied by design, not violated: repo doesn't run shellcheck -> C-003's conditional is trivially met; the mission over-delivered by running it manually.

(3) The load-bearing FR-005 criterion (authored, tracked, read-only GET-only, 5 subcommands) is proven by static grep (no POST/PUT/DELETE/-X) + stubbed-curl call-log (every call --get) + 20 token-free pytest tests (FR-006). "shellcheck clean" is a supplementary note; removing it changes no verdict. The security-relevant read-only property has independent, stronger enforcement than a lint tool.

(4) NFR-002 already confirms no "shellcheck disable" directives (confirmed_absent), independently verified — consistent with a genuine clean run.

(5) The "security / gate enforcement" severity is inflated: shellcheck is a lint/style tool, not a security gate. The inability to re-witness (shellcheck absent in this env) is a reviewer-environment limitation, not proof the claim is false.

(6) The recommendation (add a CI shellcheck gate) is scope creep: 12+ tracked .sh files, none shellchecked in CI; C-001 scopes the mission to a 3-part slice.

(7) Already adjudicated: .gates/post-plan.json litigated this exact shellcheck-enforcement concern, refuted the medium framing, and labeled the residue "at most LOW-severity plan-wording optimism ... already hedged by C-003's conditional — insufficient to sustain the finding as framed." This re-litigates a falsified hypothesis with no new evidence (DIR-003).

The residual truth — a careless reader could over-read "shellcheck clean" — is at most a cosmetic LOW wording nicety, already hedged by C-003's explicit conditional, and does not clearly withstand the default-refute standard as a real pre-merge finding.
